channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: How did a rare strain of Ebola outrun doctors?
item.description: How a rare Ebola strain spread across two countries before anyone knew about it.

item.title: Why the SpaceX IPO is the talk of Wall Street and beyond
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket company is expected to make the biggest public debut in history.

item.title: Timelapse shows Hajj pilgrims performing Tawaf in Mecca
item.description: Muslims from around the world are gathering in Saudi Arabia for the annual Hajj pilgrimage.

item.title: Iran war day 87: Trump says US not in rush to sign deal, dashing optimism
item.description: Iranian media are reporting that a deal may not be reached amid disagreements on some key issues.

item.title: Rubio says US will find ‘another way’ if Iran talks fail
item.description: US secretary of state says a 'pretty solid' deal is on the table in terms of opening up the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Gaza flotilla activists return to Australia, describing abuse
item.description: Australian activists from the Gaza-bound aid flotilla have arrived back in Sydney, reuniting with loved ones.

item.title: Rubio: ‘Trump’s not going to make a bad deal’
item.description: Rubio: 'Trump's not going to make a bad deal'

item.title: Singapore’s economy beats expectations as gov’t warns of Iran war fallout
item.description: GDP grows 6 percent year-on-year in first quarter as AI boom outweighs rising energy costs.

item.title: Mercedes teen Antonelli wins Canadian GP for fourth straight F1 victory
item.description: Antonelli finished more than 10 seconds ahead of Ferrari’s Hamilton, who secured his second podium finish in 2026.

item.title: Wembanyama scores 33 as Spurs rip Thunder to level NBA playoffs
item.description: NBA Western Conference Finals level at 2-2 after San Antonio Spurs thrash defending champions Oklahoma City 103-82.

item.title: Guardiola delivers emotional farewell as Manchester City manager
item.description: Outgoing Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola spoke about the memories built during his decade at the club

item.title: Venezuelan prisoners stage rooftop protest over alleged abuse and shootings
item.description: Inmates in Venezuela's western Barinas prison staged a protest over alleged abuse.

item.title: Africa Day 2026: Has the continent achieved true liberation?
item.description: Africa Day 2026 spotlights power, debt and digital control as Africans redefine sovereignty.

item.title: Mexico City paints record-breaking football mural ahead of World Cup
item.description: Mexico City unveiled a mural that earned a Guinness World Record ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Russia invites media to view deadly strike on college in Luhansk
item.description: Russia provided a rare look at damage caused by a Ukrainian strike on a college in occupied Luhansk

item.title: Death toll rises to four in Philippines building collapse; 17 missing
item.description: Rescuers search for survivors after thermal scans indicate breathing and heartbeats beneath debris.

item.title: Oil prices fall amid mixed signals on US-Iran peace deal
item.description: Japan's stock market surges to record high on hopes of an end to US-Israel war on Iran.

item.title: Syria holds legislative elections in former Kurdish-controlled areas
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Heidi Pett reports on the Syrian legislative elections in Hasakah and the town of Kobane.

item.title: LIVE: Iran says agreement with US ‘not imminent’ despite progress
item.description: US president says any potential agreement with Tehran will be 'good and proper' as mediation efforts continue.

item.title: Ecuador’s Noboa pledges to extradite criminals in State of the Union speech
item.description: The right-wing president highlighted anti-crime operations and economic progress, while critics warned of abuses.

item.title: Cuba thanks China for rice shipment amid worsening humanitarian conditions
item.description: The Caribbean island has faced a fuel blockade that has cut off essential supplies, as Trump seeks regime change.

item.title: Inside a Beirut barbershop shaped by war and crisis
item.description: For nearly 20 years, Mario Habib has run a barbershop through wars, economic collapse and political crisis in Lebanon.

item.title: After San Diego shooting, Muslim Americans aim to turn grief into action
item.description: More than 25,000 US Muslims have come together for the annual ICNA conference, where speakers encouraged activism.

item.title: Pakistan’s Eid livestock traders losing as war on Iran pushes up prices
item.description: Livestock traders in Pakistan say the war on Iran has hurt their sales ahead of Eid al-Adha.

item.title: Senegal parliament speaker steps down as political crisis worsens
item.description: The move by speaker El Malick Ndiaye clears the way for sacked premier Ousmane Sonko to run for head of parliament.
